comments: Scope: reviewed every hunk of f01d662..713526e (12 files, +1956/-71): new crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/small_file_probe.rs (782 lines), instrumentation call sites in pipeline.rs, sink.rs, transfer_session/data_plane.rs, transfer_session/mod.rs, session_client.rs, transfer_session/local.rs, transfer/mod.rs re-exports, +445 test lines in crates/blit-core/tests/transfer_session_roles.rs, .review/findings/otp12-pf1-p2-observer.md, REVIEW.md index line, and the 4-line docs/STATE.md update. No material issue found; STATE.md claims match the code (no performance evidence claimed, H6/H7 not graded).

Default-off verified at every construction site: SmallFileProbe::default() carries no emitter and only arms via or_from_env requiring BLIT_TRACE_SMALL_FILE_PROBE=1 truthy AND non-empty BLIT_TRACE_RUN_ID (unit guard env_activation_requires_the_canonical_nonempty_run_id); run_push_session/run_pull_session_with_client use Default::default() (session_client.rs:178,341); run_local_session and its test explicitly use SmallFileProbe::disabled() at both ends (local.rs:618-631,896-909); capture() sets allow_env=false.

Observer bias/inertness, parity, and fidelity are directly guarded: small_file_probe_is_complete_and_inert_across_roles_and_carriers (tests/transfer_session_roles.rs:2244) runs OFF vs ON across both initiator roles and both carriers (TCP + in-stream, 256 files) and asserts equal summaries, needed_paths, data_plane_streams, byte-identical trees and metadata, exactly 2 reports per session (one per semantic endpoint with SessionPhaseRole endpoint/initiator roles), shared correlation_id, receive-shard/sink-shard joins on keyed shard ids, non-vacuous TimingAggregates, and FILE_COUNT-consistent bookkeeping inventory.

Material-quality checks passed: bounded output (MAX_SHARD_RECORDS=16_384 with shard_receive_dropped/shard_sink_dropped counters); single-shot finish via AtomicBool::swap(AcqRel) preserving zero fields; correlation integrity via keyed blake3 digests (TCP session-token key; in-stream run-ID key with the per-session run-ID uniqueness requirement documented in code, finding record, and STATE.md); timing-boundary honesty in field docs (record_receive_ns includes transport wait and carrier-specific start boundaries — TCP includes tar header, in-stream starts after TarShardHeader validation/claim; correlation_ns excluded from total_ns; close documented as fd-drop, not fsync); historical-backport usability (schema field, sync-wait vs map-op splits documented with historical equivalents, zero-field preservation guard).

Independent guard proof executed in this retained worktree (ptk unavailable; direct cargo): GREEN — cargo test -p blit-core --lib small_file_probe: 3/3; cargo test -p blit-core --test transfer_session_roles small_file_probe_is_complete_and_inert: 1/1. MUTATION (documented class: constant shard IDs) — production shard_id (small_file_probe.rs:597-605) replaced with a constant string, test code untouched. RED for the predicted reason — shard_ids_are_stable_ordered_and_key_isolated failed at the cross-run assert_ne! (small_file_probe.rs:748) with left == right == "constant-shard-id". RESTORE — noninteractive git checkout of the file; worktree blob hash 181975bac4ef8df43325b40f4de32b5963c7149b equals git blob at 713526e:crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/small_file_probe.rs. GREEN again 3/3 + 1/1; git status --porcelain empty; HEAD = 713526e8f4cbe61f881a24d1f19cc481d0a8b188. Worktree left retained and clean; no commits, branches, pushes, or deletions.
